rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Will you give this shirt tomorrow ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: I have to attend a wedding.

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: If you had told me yesterday. I would have stitched.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Had I known yesterday, I would have told.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Why didn't you bring the book to-day ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: If it were not a bank-holiday, I would have brought.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Will you bring tomorrow without fail ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: naale beere kelsa ide, aadruu heegaadruu maadi tartiini.
rows.english: Tomorrow, I have some other work, but still I will bring it some how.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Why did you not prepare sambar to-day ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: If you had brought vegetables, I would have prepared.

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: niivu heeliddre tartidde. hoogli, naale tartiini maadi.
rows.english: If you had told me, I would have brought. Its o. k. I will bring tomorrow. You may prepare it then.
